title: kummerowia stipulacea
intro: Kummerowia stipulacea, also known as Japanese clover or creeping thyme, is a type of flowering plant native to East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Kummerowia stipulacea include soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. Kummerowia stipulacea can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in traditional East Asian medicine to promote overall well-being.
